LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EILEEN CHAN'S DEMURRER TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLOW MR. LINTON IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH TO ALLEGE A NON-TIME BARRED CLAIM AGAINST MS. CHAN AND THE FACTUAL REASON WHY HE DID NOT DISCOVER SUCH CLAIM UNTIL A PERIOD OF TIME LESS THAT 3 YEARS PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE LAWSUIT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EILEEN CHAN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DARNICE LINTON IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE JANUARY 25, 2017 ORDER SUSTAINING MS. CHAN'S DEMURRER TO MR. LINTON'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT GAVE PERMISSION TO MR. LINTON TO FILE A NON-TIME BARRED CLAIM AGAINST MS. CHAN AND THE FACTUAL REASONS HE DID DISCOVER SUCH CLAIMS UNTIL A PERIOD OF TIME LESS THAN THREE YEARS PRIOR TO HIS FILING THIS LAWSUIT. MR. LINTON'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE SUCH A CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Continued To Jun-21-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Darnice Linton, in pro per, Tel. 510.978.8500, for plaintiffs;
John E. Cowan, of Law Offices of John E. Cowan, Tel. 888.252.1231, for defendants Chan.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EILEEN CHAN's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT filed by plaintiff Darnice Linton is sustained without leave to amend. The January 25, 2017 order sustaining Ms. Chan's demurrer to Mr. Linton's first amended complaint gave permission to Mr. Linton to file a non-time barred claim against Ms. Chan and the factual reasons he did discover such claims until a period of time less than three years prior to his filing this lawsuit. Mr. Linton's second amended complaint fails to allege such a claim. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Continued To Apr-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DARNICE LINTON, TEL. NO. 1-510-978-3500, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JOHN E. COWAN, TEL. NO. 1-888-252-1231 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. Defendant Eileen Chan's demurrer to the sole cause of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend to allow Mr. Linton if he can do so in good faith to allege a non-time barred claim against Ms. Chan and the factual reason why he did not discover such claim until a period of time less that 3 years prior to the filing of the lawsuit. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-28-2016 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Darnice Linton telephone number 510-978-8500 appeared in Propria Persona.
John Cowan of Law Offices of John E. Cowan telephone number 888-252-1231 for Defendant Eileen Chan.

The Court explained the tentative ruling to Mr. Linton. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Eileen Chan's demurrer to both the general negligence and fraud causes of action in the complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the general negligence cause of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action. Plaintiff Darnice Linton contends that he discovered Ms. Chan's alleged wrongful conduct on October 24, 2012. Even if proved, that would still mean his general negligence cause of action is time-barred since it is governed by a two year limitations statute. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Linton is given leave to file a first amended complaint alleging only a fraud cause of action based on alleged wrongful conduct by Ms. Chan that he did not discover and could not reasonably have discovered until three years or less from the date he filed this lawsuit. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
